rulings.Case Title: CHERYL HOSKINS et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBIRT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Deem Plaintiffs' Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set Two Admitted, Or In The Alternative, Move To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: ON CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, APRIL 22, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:00AM, LINE 1. DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S Motion To Deem Plaintiffs' Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set Two Admitted, Or In The Alternative, Move To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s motion to compel further responses to request for admissions, set two, is granted as to requests 48 and 50-55. The requests are relevant on the issue of Proposition 51 allocation and are not vague, ambiguous, burdensome, harassing, make-work or of no utility. Request 49 is vague because Bartells Abestos Settlement Trust is a trust and not a traditional defendant. To either admit or deny that asbestos fibers attributable to the Trust were a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff¿s injury could potentially be misleading. As to Request 49, the parties are ordered to meet and confer in order to write a request that recognizes Bartells Asbestos Settlement Trust identity as a trust. If the parties cannot agree on a revised version of request 49, a hearing is required on that issue. Both Plaintiff and Defendant¿s requests for sanctions are denied. = (220/HEK)
